title: GDS 3.0 · Consolidation
content:
## Announcing release of Green Core 3.0

Today we release version 3.0 of the Green Core library. This release contains name and value changes of certain design tokens, additions of design tokens, typography naming convention changes, removal of previous deprecations and a few property renames. These changes are part of the continuous refinement work to sustain long-term health and scalability of the design system.  

You can find the full list of code changes in the [changelog entry on Github](https://seb.ghe.com/sebgroup/green-design-system-green/releases/tag/%40sebgroup%2Fgreen-core%403.0.0 "https://seb.ghe.com/sebgroup/green-design-system-green/releases/tag/%40sebgroup%2fgreen-core%403.0.0"), and [a new migration guide is also available in Storybook.](https://storybook.seb.io/latest/core/?path=/docs/guides-migration-guides--docs "https://storybook.seb.io/latest/core/?path=/docs/guides-migration-guides--docs")

### Breaking changes

As mentioned in the previous announcement about 3.0, we have assigned this release a **Level 2** (Moderate changes) grade on the impact scale. This means most of the consumer-side changes consists of symbol name changes that can largely be handled via search-and-replace, and should not require and structural or logic changes.  

### Migration automation

To make updating as smooth as possible, 3.0 adds a new migration automation CLI. This is now shipped as part of the Core Context CLI, and is primarily intended to help your coding agent perform the upgrade for you, but it can be run manually as well.  

Here’s how it works:

Each breaking change includes a "migration unit", which consists of a text description of the change, before and after examples, as well as a set of regular expressions (or other suitable mechanism) that can be run to procedurally update the code base. It will also detect and flag less clear-cut cases, such as dynamic assignments where a simple replace may not be safe. This can then be handled by the agent (or manually by a human). Please check the migration guide linked above for details on how to use it.  

We’re hoping that this setup will cover most of the upgrade work, even in more complex code bases.  

### Older code bases

We also added a set of migration units for 2.x, so if you are still using 1.x of Green Core and would like to go straight to 3.0, you should be able to do that by running migrations for 2.x first, and for 3.x.  

### Legacy React and Angular libs

Both of these libraries depend on Green Core, and as a result both of them has gotten a major bump as well.

[https://seb.ghe.com/sebgroup/green-design-system-green/releases/tag/%40sebgroup%2Fgreen-react%405.0…](https://seb.ghe.com/sebgroup/green-design-system-green/releases/tag/%40sebgroup%2Fgreen-react%405.0…)

[https://seb.ghe.com/sebgroup/green-design-system-green/releases/tag/%40sebgroup%2Fgreen-angular%401…](https://seb.ghe.com/sebgroup/green-design-system-green/releases/tag/%40sebgroup%2Fgreen-angular%401…)  

### Help

If you run in to any trouble upgrading, please reach out in the [support](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/19%3Afd0392b84d2044fe8ef49fabc78f7ba7%40thread.tacv2/Support?groupId=3ad3eb8f-ab23-44e6-9b61-d08fee172513&tenantId=9a8ff9e3-0e35-4620-a724-e9834dc50b51 "https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/19%3afd0392b84d2044fe8ef49fabc78f7ba7%40thread.tacv2/support?groupid=3ad3eb8f-ab23-44e6-9b61-d08fee172513&tenantid=9a8ff9e3-0e35-4620-a724-e9834dc50b51") channel! And as always, you are welcome to book [Green Coaching](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/entity/0d820ecd-def2-4297-adad-78056cde7c78/_djb2_msteams_prefix_545191778?context=%7B%22channelId%22%3A%2219%3A33f450cae2764531930c4eca19aa4c26%40thread.tacv2%22%7D&tenantId=9a8ff9e3-0e35-4620-a724-e9834dc50b51 "https://teams.microsoft.com/l/entity/0d820ecd-def2-4297-adad-78056cde7c78/_djb2_msteams_prefix_545191778?context=%7b%22channelid%22%3a%2219%3a33f450cae2764531930c4eca19aa4c26%40thread.tacv2%22%7d&tenantid=9a8ff9e3-0e35-4620-a724-e9834dc50b51") on Mondays to get hands-on help with any Green-related issues you are facing.

Thank you for your patience, support and feedback!
